rawHead:
<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1" />
<title>General relativity</title>
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.css">
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.js"></script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/contrib/auto-render.min.js"></script>
<script>
(function(){
  function renderAll(){
    if(typeof renderMathInElement!=='function'){ return setTimeout(renderAll,50); }
    renderMathInElement(document.body,{
      delimiters:[
        {left:'$$',right:'$$',display:true},
        {left:'$',right:'$',display:false},
        {left:'\\(',right:'\\)',display:false},
        {left:'\\[',right:'\\]',display:true}
      ],
      throwOnError:false,
      macros:{
        '\\Spec':'\\Spec',
        '\\Gal':'\\Gal',
        '\\Hom':'\\Hom',
        '\\tr':'\\tr',
        '\\ad':'\\ad',
        '\\ind':'\\ind'
      }
    });
  }
  if(document.readyState==='loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded',renderAll);
  else renderAll();
})();
</script>
<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;
    --ink:#e8ecf1; --mute:#9aa4b2; --line:#2a3242;
    --yellow:#ffd866; --blue:#58c4dd; --green:#83c167;
    --pink:#e07a5f; --violet:#b39ddb; --cyan:#7de0d6;
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}
  html,body{margin:0;padding:0;background:var(--bg);color:var(--ink);
    font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Inter,Roboto,sans-serif;
    font-size:17px;line-height:1.65}
  a{color:var(--blue)}
  code{font-family:ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;
    background:#0b0f16;padding:1px 6px;border-radius:4px;color:#f0c987;font-size:0.92em}
  main{max-width:880px;margin:0 auto;padding:4rem 1.5rem 8rem}
  h1{font-size:2.4rem;letter-spacing:-0.01em;margin:0 0 0.4rem}
  h2{font-size:1.6rem;margin:3.2rem 0 0.8rem;color:#fff;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding-bottom:.4rem}
  h3{font-size:1.15rem;margin:1.8rem 0 .6rem;color:var(--yellow)}
  p{margin:0.7rem 0}
  .sub{color:var(--mute);margin-top:0;margin-bottom:2rem}
  .widget{background:var(--panel);border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:10px;
    padding:1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem;margin:1.2rem 0}
  .widget .hd{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;margin-bottom:.6rem;gap:.8rem;flex-wrap:wrap}
  .widget .hd .ttl{font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.95rem;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:uppercase}
  .widget .hd .hint{color:var(--mute);font-size:.85rem}
  .row{display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;margin:.4rem 0}
  .row label{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem}
  input[type="text"],input[type="number"],select{
    background:#0b0f16;color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .55rem;font-family:inherit;font-size:.95rem}
  input[type="range"]{accent-color:var(--yellow);flex:1;max-width:260px}
  button{background:var(--panel2);color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .7rem;cursor:pointer;font:inherit}
  button:hover{background:#242c3e}
  button.active{background:var(--yellow);color:#111;border-color:var(--yellow)}
  .readout{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:6px;
    padding:.6rem .8rem;font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.92rem;color:#cbd5e1;
    min-height:1.6em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word}
  .pill{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:999px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:.82rem;color:var(--mute);margin-right:4px}
  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  /* sticky sidebar TOC (wide screens only; narrow screens fall back to top-nav) */
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute);margin:0 0 .5rem;padding-bottom:.35rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}
  aside.sidetoc ol{list-style:none;padding:0;margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc li{margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc a{display:block;padding:.3rem .5rem;color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none;
    border-left:2px solid transparent;line-height:1.35;
    transition:color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease}
  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}
  @media (max-width:1180px){ aside.sidetoc{display:none} }
  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }
  .hero{padding:3rem 0 1rem}
  .small{font-size:.88rem;color:var(--mute)}
  footer{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem;text-align:center;margin-top:4rem}
  table.plain{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.95rem;margin:.6rem 0}
  table.plain th,table.plain td{padding:.35rem .5rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);text-align:left}
  table.plain th{color:var(--mute);font-weight:500}
  /* callback-css-auto-begin */
  aside.callback{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:rgba(88,196,221,0.05);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--cyan) 45%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.callback .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.callback ul{margin:0;padding-left:1.1rem}
  aside.callback ul li{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
/* callback-css-auto-end */
  details.changelog{
    margin:2rem 0 1rem;padding:.6rem .9rem;
    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);
    border-radius:6px;
    font-size:.85rem;
  }
  details.changelog summary{
    cursor:pointer;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:.7rem;color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);
  }
  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 6%, transparent);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 55%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}
  aside.related a{color:inherit}
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-begin */
  html[data-hide-widgets] .widget { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-quizzes] .quiz { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-lineage] .lineage-strip { display: none !important; }
  .mv-display-toggles { display: inline-flex; gap: .6rem; align-items: center; }
  .mv-display-toggle { background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--line, #333); border-radius: 6px; padding: 5px 10px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1; color: inherit; transition: background 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease; }
  .mv-display-toggle:hover { background: var(--panel, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)); border-color: var(--mute, #666); }
  .mv-display-toggle--off { opacity: 0.35; }
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-end */
</style>
<!-- breadcrumb-head-auto-begin -->

rawBodyPrefix:
← Notebook
1 Spacetime 2 Einstein equations 3 Schwarzschild 4 Kerr 5 FLRW 6 Waves

General relativity

Spacetime is geometry. Curvature is gravity. The Einstein equations couple the two — and from them fall black holes, an expanding universe, and gravitational waves.

blocks.html:
1. Spacetime as a Lorentzian manifold

A spacetime is a smooth 4-manifold $M$ together with a non-degenerate metric $g$ of Lorentzian signature $(-,+,+,+)$. Locally we can pick coordinates so the line element looks like Minkowski:

$$ds^2 \;=\; -\,dt^2 + dx^2 + dy^2 + dz^2.$$

The minus sign in front of $dt^2$ does all the physics. At each point $p\in M$ the quadratic form $g_p$ partitions tangent vectors into three classes:

  • Timelike ($g(v,v)<0$) — possible 4-velocity of a massive observer.
  • Lightlike / null ($g(v,v)=0$) — direction of a photon's worldline.
  • Spacelike ($g(v,v)>0$) — directions inside a single instant of simultaneity.

The set of null vectors forms a double cone — the light cone — which encodes causal structure: information can only flow along causal (timelike or null) curves inside the future cone.

Light cones & vector classes
drag the vector head; classification is live
drag the orange tip…

The light cone is more than a picture — it is the only part of geometry that is invariant under boosts. A Lorentz transformation tilts time and space axes but maps null lines to null lines. Causality (which events can influence which) is the diffeomorphism-invariant content of a Lorentzian metric.

See also
  • Riemannian metrics

Used in
General relativity · Einstein field equations
</section>

2. Einstein field equations

On a Riemannian or Lorentzian manifold the Levi-Civita connection produces the Riemann curvature tensor $R^\rho{}_{\sigma\mu\nu}$, its Ricci trace $R_{\mu\nu}=R^\rho{}_{\mu\rho\nu}$, and the scalar curvature $R=g^{\mu\nu}R_{\mu\nu}$. Einstein's field equations couple these geometric objects to the matter stress-energy tensor $T_{\mu\nu}$:

$$\boxed{\; R_{\mu\nu} \;-\; \tfrac12\,g_{\mu\nu}\,R \;+\; \Lambda\,g_{\mu\nu} \;=\; 8\pi G\, T_{\mu\nu} \;}$$

The left-hand side $G_{\mu\nu}=R_{\mu\nu}-\tfrac12 g_{\mu\nu}R$ is the Einstein tensor; the contracted Bianchi identity $\nabla^\mu G_{\mu\nu}=0$ is automatic, which forces conservation $\nabla^\mu T_{\mu\nu}=0$ on the matter side. As John Wheeler put it: spacetime tells matter how to move; matter tells spacetime how to curve.

Curvature ↔ matter dial
scrub the matter density and the Ricci sketch responds
…

Tracing the equation gives $-R+4\Lambda = 8\pi G\,T$, so vacuum with $\Lambda=0$ collapses to Ricci-flat: $R_{\mu\nu}=0$. That is a quasilinear hyperbolic system in 10 unknowns $g_{\mu\nu}$; diffeomorphism gauge subtracts 4, leaving 6 propagating degrees of freedom — the seed of gravitational waves.

See also
  • Classification: elliptic / parabolic / hyperbolic
  • Ricci curvature and Einstein manifolds

Used in
General relativity · FLRW cosmology
General relativity · Linearised gravity and gravitational waves
General relativity · Schwarzschild solution and event horizons
</section>

3. Schwarzschild & event horizons

The first nontrivial vacuum solution Schwarzschild found in 1916 — months after Einstein wrote down the field equations — is also the unique static spherically symmetric solution (Birkhoff's theorem):

$$ds^2 \;=\; -\Big(1-\tfrac{2M}{r}\Big)dt^2 \;+\; \Big(1-\tfrac{2M}{r}\Big)^{-1}\!dr^2 \;+\; r^2\bigl(d\theta^2+\sin^2\!\theta\,d\varphi^2\bigr).$$

The metric component $g_{tt}=-(1-2M/r)$ vanishes at $r=2M$ — the event horizon. This is a coordinate singularity (the Kretschmann scalar $R_{\mu\nu\rho\sigma}R^{\mu\nu\rho\sigma}=48 M^2/r^6$ is finite there), but a causal singularity: light cones tip over and inside $r<2M$ all future-directed worldlines hit $r=0$, where curvature genuinely diverges.

Tipping light cones
drag the slider for radius; cones flip past r = 2M
…

Outside the horizon Schwarzschild geodesics reproduce the precession of Mercury's perihelion and the bending of starlight — the two classical tests Einstein cited in 1915.

See also
  • Levi-Civita connection and geodesics

Used in
General relativity · Kerr metric and rotating black holes
</section>

4. Kerr metric & the ergosphere

Real astrophysical black holes spin. The unique stationary axisymmetric vacuum solution is Roy Kerr's 1963 metric, parametrised by mass $M$ and angular momentum $J=Ma$. In Boyer–Lindquist coordinates two surfaces emerge:

  • The event horizon at $r_+ = M + \sqrt{M^2 - a^2}$.
  • The outer stationary-limit surface at $r_{\rm SL}(\theta) = M + \sqrt{M^2 - a^2\cos^2\theta}$, where $g_{tt}=0$.

Between them sits the ergosphere: $\partial_t$ is spacelike there, so no observer can sit at fixed $(r,\theta,\varphi)$ — frame dragging compels co-rotation with the hole. The Penrose process exploits this to extract rotational energy.

Kerr horizons
slide the spin a/M from 0 (Schwarzschild) to 1 (extremal)
…

Inside the ergosphere a particle can carry negative Killing energy as measured at infinity. Drop a fragment with negative energy into the hole and the surviving piece exits with more energy than the original — at the cost of the hole's spin. This is Penrose's mining scheme.
</section>

5. FLRW cosmology & dark energy

If we postulate that the universe is spatially homogeneous and isotropic on the largest scales, the metric must take the Friedmann–Lemaître–Robertson–Walker form:

$$ds^2 \;=\; -dt^2 \;+\; a(t)^2\!\left[\frac{dr^2}{1-kr^2} + r^2\!\left(d\theta^2 + \sin^2\!\theta\,d\varphi^2\right)\right],$$

with a single scale factor $a(t)$ and a discrete spatial-curvature parameter $k\in\{-1,0,+1\}$. Plugging into the Einstein equations with a perfect-fluid stress-energy $T_{\mu\nu} = (\rho+p)u_\mu u_\nu + p\,g_{\mu\nu}$ gives the Friedmann equations:

$$\left(\frac{\dot a}{a}\right)^2 = \frac{8\pi G}{3}\rho - \frac{k}{a^2} + \frac{\Lambda}{3}, \qquad \frac{\ddot a}{a} = -\frac{4\pi G}{3}(\rho + 3p) + \frac{\Lambda}{3}.$$

Different fluids drive different expansion histories: matter ($p=0$) gives $a\propto t^{2/3}$, radiation ($p=\rho/3$) gives $a\propto t^{1/2}$, and a cosmological constant ($p=-\rho$) gives exponential expansion $a\propto e^{Ht}$ — the late-time accelerating universe.

Scale factor a(t)
scrub the dark-energy fraction ΩΛ; matter fills the rest
…

The current cosmological-constant best fit is $\Omega_\Lambda \approx 0.7$, $\Omega_m \approx 0.3$ — so we live in the moment when matter and dark energy contribute comparably, and the expansion has just transitioned from decelerating to accelerating.
</section>

6. Gravitational waves

Linearise Einstein's equations around Minkowski: write $g_{\mu\nu} = \eta_{\mu\nu} + h_{\mu\nu}$ with $|h|\ll 1$, and let $\bar h_{\mu\nu} = h_{\mu\nu} - \tfrac12 \eta_{\mu\nu} h$ be the trace-reversed perturbation. In Lorenz gauge $\partial^\mu \bar h_{\mu\nu}=0$ the field equations collapse to a wave equation:

$$\Box\,\bar h_{\mu\nu} \;=\; -16\pi G\, T_{\mu\nu}.$$

Using the residual gauge freedom we land in transverse-traceless gauge, where only two physical polarisations survive: the "plus" mode $h_+$ and the "cross" mode $h_\times$. A passing wave alternately stretches one transverse direction while squeezing the other.

Plus & cross polarisations
choose mode and play; ring of test masses deforms
…

On 14 September 2015 LIGO recorded a 0.2-second chirp of strain $h\sim 10^{-21}$ — the inspiral and merger of two black holes of $\sim 30 M_\odot$ at $\sim 1.3$ Gly. Direct detection of gravitational waves, a century after Einstein's prediction.

See also
  • Wave equation and d'Alembert

Connections

GR is the home of nearly every open problem at the boundary of geometry, analysis, and physics. Black-hole stability, cosmic censorship, the well-posedness of the Cauchy problem in the large, quantum gravity, and the dark-sector content of cosmology are all live research fronts. Beneath each: a Lorentzian PDE whose nonlinearity refuses to be tamed.

  • Riemannian geometry — Ricci curvature — vacuum Einstein is exactly the Ricci-flat condition; Lorentzian signature is the only twist.
  • PDEs — wave equation — linearised gravity is a tensor wave equation; Cauchy stability of GR is its nonlinear analogue.
  • Differential geometry — fundamental forms — induced metrics on Cauchy hypersurfaces and their second fundamental form give the ADM initial-value formulation.
  • Ricci flow — sister flow $\partial_t g = -2 R_{\mu\nu}$ uses curvature as a heat operator; Perelman's solution of geometrisation borrows GR techniques.
  • Symplectic manifolds — phase-space formulation of GR (the constraint algebra) sits at the gateway to canonical quantum gravity.
